content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 23, 1991

Robert D. Winston
KIRSHER, WINSTON & BOSTON
219 Main Street
P. O. Box 669
Weed, CA 96094

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-176

Dear Mr. Winston:

This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding your duties and responsibilities under the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Our
advice is prospective in nature. We make no comments regarding
past conduct. (Regulation 18329(c)(4)(A), copy enclosed.)

QUESTION

Do the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act disqualify
you from participating in decisions regarding a request for
reconsideration submitted to the city council by an applicant for
a sewer connection?

CONCLUSION

The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not
disqualify you from participating in decisions regarding a request
for reconsideration submitted to the city council by an applicant
for a sewer connection.

FACTS

You are the city attorney for the City of Mount Shasta. An
applicant for a city sewer connection appeared before the city

_______
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. A-91-176
Page 2


council. The connection is necessary in order for the applicant
to obtain approval of his subdivision located outside the city
limits. You have not provided legal advice regarding this matter.
However, you asseted the city council with procedural questions
during the council's discussion of this matter and prepared the
resolution adopted subsequent to the council's decision to allow
the connection. A request for reconsideration of this matter is
now pending before the city council.

Your spouse has discussed with the applicant the possibility
of purchasing one of the applicant's parcels at such time as the
subdivision is approved. Your spouse would build an office for
her wholly-owned business upon this parcel. No written or verbal
agreement regarding this matter has been reached.

ANALYSIS

Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making,
participating in, or using their official position to influence a
governmental deicision in which they know or have reason to know
they have a financial interest. As city attorney for the City of
Mount Shasta, you are a public official. (Section 82048.)

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the
meaning of section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, or on a
member of the official's immediate family,² or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars ($250) or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

_______________
2 An official's "immediate family" means the official's spouse
and dependent children. (Section 82029.)

Our File No. A-91-176
Page 3


(e)  Any donor of, or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value
provided to, received by, or promised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.

     For purposes of this section, indirect
investment or interest means any investment or
interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of
a public official, by an agent on behalf of a
public official, or by a business entity or trust
in which the official, the official's agents,
spouse, and dependent children own directly,
indirectly, or beneficially a 10-percent interest
or greater.

                         Section 87103.

     You have indicated that your spouse discussed with the
applicant the possibility of purchasing a parcel in the
applicant's subdivision.  This discussion is not sufficient to
create a financial interest requiring your disqualification.  Your
facts do not show that you have purchased the parcel or that you
have received income in excess of $250 from the applicant for the
sewer connection.  Therefore, based upon the information provided,
the Act does not require your disqualification from participating
in any governmental decision regarding this matter.

     For your future reference, we are enclosing a pamphlet
entitled "California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public
Officials" which provides information regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Act.  Should you have any further
questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at
(916) 322-5901.

                              Sincerely,

                              Scott Hallabrin
                              Acting General Counsel


                              By:  Blanca M. Breeze
                                   Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BMB:aa

Enclosure

council. The connection is necessary in order for the applicant to obtain approval of his subdivision located outside the city limits. You have not provided legal advice regarding this matter. However, you assisted the city council with procedural questions during the council's discussion of this matter and prepared the resolution adopted subsequent to the council's decision to allow the connection. Your spouse would build an office for her wholly-owned business upon this parcel. No written or verbal agreement regarding this matter has been reached.
sections.analysis:
Section 87100 prohibits public officials from making, participating in, or using their official position to influence a governmental decision in which they know or have reason to know they have a financial interest. As city attorney for the City of Mount Shasta, you are a public official. (Section 82048.)

An official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the official, or on a member of the official's immediate family,2 or on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management.

2 An official's "immediate family" means the official's spouse and dependent children. (Section 82029.)

(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.

For purposes of this section, indirect investment or interest means any investment or interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public official, by an agent on behalf of a public official, or by a business entity or trust in which the official, the official's agents, spouse, and dependent children own directly, indirectly, or beneficially a 10-percent interest or greater.

Section 87103.

You have indicated that your spouse discussed with the applicant the possibility of purchasing a parcel in the applicant's subdivision. This discussion is not sufficient to create a financial interest requiring your disqualification. Your facts do not show that you have purchased the parcel or that you have received income in excess of $250 from the applicant for the sewer connection. Therefore, based upon the information provided, the Act does not require your disqualification from participating in any governmental decision regarding this matter.

For your future reference, we are enclosing a pamphlet entitled "California's Conflict of Interest Law for Public Officials" which provides information regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act. Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

QUESTION: Do the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act disqualify you from participating in decisions regarding a request for reconsideration submitted to the city council by an applicant for a sewer connection?

CONCLUSION: The conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act do not disqualify you from participating in decisions regarding a request for reconsideration submitted to the city council by an applicant for a sewer connection.
